label: About
title: About Dragonfly Field Shrine for a Native Front Yard
short_blurb: This edition began with 1,392 recent Markdown notes, 30 selected signals, and 12 inspected source links. The final image is a scene-first rendering of those materials, not a template page.
body: The daily run started in Nick's vault and selected notes around LangMem for agents, AuraJS restart evaluation for Swagbound, and Southeast Michigan Front Yard Native Garden. It found 126 possible source links; this edition uses saved URLs from Homegrown National Park, Michigan Flora, Wild Ones, and Detroit Land Lab for its windows.

For the visual seed, the process used Get Started Planting Native Plants - Homegrown National Park and its page preview image from homegrownnationalpark.org. GPT-5.5 translated that source image into a macro, rain-wet native garden scene with low late-morning sun, pale green bokeh, weathered cedar, pressed botanical paper, translucent dragonfly wings, mauve seed pods, damp clay soil, brushed brass labels, milkweed silk, and mossed stone.

gpt-image-2 rendered the plate as a small Southeast Michigan front-yard field shrine: a painted native planting wall, seed envelope rack, dry-mesic-wet clay ground map, brass plant markers, Detroit Land Lab stones, a water basin, a Wild Ones garden scroll, and a Michigan Flora field ledger.

After the image existed, a GPT-5.5 vision pass inspected the actual plate instead of trusting the prompt. It found 8 objects and 10 usable surfaces, including the painted Native Plants of Southeast Michigan wall, cedar rack of local seed envelopes, three-zone clay garden ground map, Detroit Land Lab engraved stones, reflective water basin, Wild Ones scroll, Michigan Flora ledger, pressed specimen sheets, and brass plant markers. Those real visible areas became the clickable regions.

The package was assembled under public/editions with source bindings, ambiance, masks, interpretation, and enhancement plans. The run artifacts are kept at tmp/daily-process-runs/daily-process-2026-04-25T22-38-12-812Z so the edition can be reviewed from the actual steps that made it.

typography.body_family: 'DFE Source Serif 4', Iowan Old Style, Georgia, serif
typography.rationale: Fraunces gives the title an organic, seed-label quality; Source Serif keeps the explanation calm and readable.
